reviews.review_text: Hit all the men, not just the men, the woman, and children too

Buying guide: cheapest way to unlock full game with all maps.

1. Wait for sale. 
2. Buy HITMAN World of Assassination for €27,99
3. HITMAN 3 Access Pass: HITMAN 2 Expansion for €6,99, this gives you access to Haven Island, New York and the Sniper Maps.

You can skip the rest, it's not worth your money unless you've finished the game and really want something new but beware, it's not that good.

- Buy the game
- Play the training
- Realize i can't play any other missions
- Spend 5 hours trying to figure out why i can't play any other mission
- Realize i bought the 30 dollar version instead of the 70 dollar version with the same name
- Throw my computer out my window

Hitman is a fantastic game, but holy hell does it make being a customer harder than it needs to be.

The FOMO around Elusive Targets is ridiculous. Why are missions and rewards still tied to limited-time events in a mostly single-player game? If I paid for the game, just let me play the content when I want.

Then there's the DLC situation. Some content has been delisted, other content is sold separately, and figuring out what actually comes with what feels like a research project. The fact that people regularly need charts, guides, and Reddit threads just to figure out what version to buy should have been a warning sign.

The game itself is excellent but the way they're selling the content is just a scam. It's intentionally confusing and intransparent to make you pay for content you either already have access to or to make you pay for content you aren't gonna recieve. The deluxe edition is the entire game anything else is just incomplete. If you're planning on actually buying this game regardless since the content is actually fire as always then buy it somewhere else even if you just buy the key.

That steam allows IO to design the steam pages like that is shocking and this should definetely be considered fraud.

Good game, horrible sales method. 

when you buy ONLY this you get access to a prologue and thats basically it. actual missions will have to be bought extra.

Hitman: World of Assassination is one of the few games that can actually keep my attention these days. As I've gotten older, I find myself getting bored with a lot of games pretty quickly, but Hitman is different. Every time I load it up, I end up playing longer than I planned.

What amazes me most is the map design. Each location feels like an entire game by itself. Whether it's a luxury resort, a crowded city street, a racetrack, or a mansion, every map is packed with details, hidden paths, secrets, and opportunities. You can tell the developers put an incredible amount of work into making each location feel alive.

The freedom is what really makes the game shine. You can complete objectives in so many different ways that no two playthroughs ever feel the same. One minute you're blending into a crowd, the next you're disguising yourself as someone completely different and setting up an elaborate accident. The amount of different scenarios and possibilities is honestly impressive.

Hitman isn't a game you rush through. It's a game that rewards curiosity, experimentation, and creativity. Even after spending hours on a single map, I'll still discover something new that I never noticed before.

If you enjoy games that let you think, plan, and approach situations your own way, this is one of the best games ever made. The level of detail in these maps still blows me away.

Maybe the definitive Hitman game and the best stealth sandbox game right now, with Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell and Metal Gear Solid basically gone.
The game is well optimized and run smoothly (around 60fps) on my low performance laptop with the lowest graphic setting.
However there are still some flaws like NPC ignoring restricted area, magic idiot bullets, gone of the human shield feature.
The game is all good and all but I had watch tutorials to buy this game still failled to get the the full game.

Great game, a lot of options of how to play & generally super fun. But the gun accuracy is kinda annoying & how many bullets the AI can take is a bit over the top. Also the missions to get repetitive after a bit, but it takes usually over a hundred hours.

7.5/10, it would be a 9 but the dlc ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ is insane & really makes IOI look greedy as hell. Do not buy it if it's not on sale.
I'm not very good at it, but I'm very much enjoying the game. The tutorial style "Story Missions" are very good at helping you figure out a level your first time.

WARNING! THE GAME DOES NOT RUN ON CURRENT AMD CARDS!

Game crashes the gpu drivers after 15-20 minutes of play. This has been a known issue for at least a year now and it has not been fixed.

i bought the game after reading everywhere online that it includes all 3 of the hitman games so i start the first ready to enjoy this supposedly great game, i played a bit of the first so i could get up to speed with the story for my youtube channel (which started on hitman 2 and is the only reason i bought this game) however i came to find out that after pressing on hitman 2 to film a video it and hitman 3 where locked behind a paywall of ANOTHER 25 pound game/dlc!!!
this is crazy considering after buying a game called hitman WORLD of assasins and looking up online a lot to see everyone and even googles ai thing said it came with ALL 3 GAMES it came with only the fist.
in no way am i blaming steam but neither am i blaming myself as this is clearly a marketing type issue, i even saw a review saying basically the same thing i did.
Well the marketing is very misleading. Bought the game and found out that i have 10% of the content
Amazing game, terrible owners. Predatory pricing worse than a nonce at a flee market.

A very good game partially ruined by IOI's decisions. Constant milking with new DLC; timed events (Elusive Targets) that should have absolutely no place in a single player title like this; time limited DLC packs aimed at exploiting players' FOMO, and so on.

saw it was on sale and bought it thinking it was hitman world of assasination. Its not. It's Hitman 1, I played and own that already. I don't mind spending the money to own the game but im surprised steam lets these anti consumer models straight up LIE to their customers about the content included. I will be refunding

So the game is good... but I bought it thinking I got a full game, instead I got about max, 10 hours of campaign. And that is by restarting each mission if I got caught. This pricing model is insane... they greatly obscure what you are actually buying here. If you buy the "base game" you are essentially buying 8-10 hours of a single chapter campaign in a "normal" video game. If you are good with that, the gameplay is pretty fun. For me, it left a sour taste in my mouth, so I won't be putting any more money into this.

For some reason I bought Hitman WOA Part 1 after playing Hitman 2, as Hitman 3 is now Hitman WOA
Of course , Part 1 is nothing.  Its not even the game.  It wont even let me play Hitman 2 from that screen.  Its 75 gb of nothing.  A total waste of money

[h1] The best Hitman ever offset by IOI at their greediest, most immoral phase [/h1]

I've been playing the Hitman series since forever, and I've lost count of the total hours I've spent in Silent Assassin, Contracts, or Blood Money. Now, [b] this isn't a review of how the WOA trilogy feels in terms of gameplay [/b]. While there's a lot I can say about it, the TLDR explanation is that despite some minor flaws (like the lack of Jesper Kyd's music, for instance), it's probably the Hitman series at its best. The problem with this new format is IOI's disgusting choice of making time-limited gameplay events/modes/ and DLCs.

[h2] Taking advantage of FOMO [/h2]

For Hitman WOA, IOI chose to implement, or should I say, manipulate its playerbase through FOMO schemes. Now, personally, I'm mostly immune to FOMO, but IOI has taken this exploitative marketing strategy to a whole new, disgustingly immoral level. Not only do you miss out on certain gameplay events completely if you don't play them when they are released/featured, but [b] you can't even buy certain DLCs because they're time-limited [/b]. Now, if this was a multiplayer-only game, these marketing practices would've been mostly accepted, but it's not. Hitman has always been a singleplayer experience, and the WOA series is no different.

Why then, do we have time-limited events and DLCs in a singleplayer-only title? There have been countless complaints about this, but unfortunately, IOI has become too big/too successful to care about what their playerbase wants. Thanks to the Hitman WOA trilogy and 007 First Light, both exceptional games on their own, they've become too big to fail, kind of like Rockstar Games. And just like Rockstar and any other greedy megacorpo, they've turned hostile towards the people who raised them to this pedestal.

[h2] What the future holds for IOI and its playerbase [/h2]

[b] "I am repeatedly amazed at how a company can make one of the greatest games ever, and then try to fight anyone who wants to play it." [/b] - Some player on Reddit (u/Kilmoore)

It seems that this tremendous success is getting to IOI's heads, and in the near future, they will turn into another Take-Two/Rockstar. Expect fan projects to be shutdown, more time-limited FOMO manipulation to come out, and worse, mods to be DMCA'd. And guess what, there will be next to no criticism. Just look at how GTA 6 fans behave. They will gladly make fun of people losing their jobs at a draconian company that openly abuses labor laws just so they can get their consumer product sooner.

One of the best games i have ever played with one of the most player friendly when it comes to additional content. 
Unfortunatly the requirement for an online connection if you want to play anything but the basic missions without achievements leads to an unsure future if they ever turn off the servers and presents an annoyance if either partys internet connection fails.

This is a good game, but I bought it because IO Interactive is advertising a reward for the upcoming 007 First Light when you buy this game, however in their article they advertise Hitman Part One at 5,99 specifically, while you have to buy the 69,99 edition instead. False advertising is not cool.
You cannot make any progress without constant internet connection. Totally unacceptable.

great gane, I am still not sure if I prefer Hitman 3's levels or Hitman 2's but all 3 games in the trilogy are great. Hitman 3's favourite missions for me were the Dubai and China levels. All levels are gorgeous with the usual multitudes of fun assassination opportunities and exploration. The story was meh and the last level was not good. The last level felt like a throwback to the final missions in the old games like Contracts and Blood money where you shoot everyone to death but this one didn't feel as great as those. Overall I think I prefer Hitman 2 slightly more but Hitman 3 is still an easy 8.5 or 9 out of 10. Hitman 2 for me is 9/10, Hitman 3 is 8.5/10 and Hitman 1 is 8/10. Great trilogy

If you want to figure out which version to get your gonna have to pay for Harvard . or watch a youtube video about it .I have an easier time doing taxes than figuring this mess out . Turned off by the whole process so It'll  probably keep me from buying the new 007 game as well

im so confused, i own world of assassination for freelancermode.... now i gotta pay an extra 100 for the game i already bought..... really should have refunded it before i played four missions in then checked for freelancer mode. Looked up hella tutorials all saying the same stuff... why do this activision all in one game ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥... no one ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ likes it dawg just ♥♥♥♥♥♥ me by making me buy the same game 10 years later... like how can  do that please i need an answer or sum besides wiz kkhalia. Been playin on and off since i was 12 and this disappoints me.
Game is fun, but currently there is a bug on AMD cards where it crashes after some time while playing. Please get this fixed.

Hitman is one of the most enjoyable stealth games I've played. I loved the freedom to approach each mission in different ways, whether through careful planning or creative improvisation. The detailed locations make exploration rewarding, and every mission feels fresh thanks to the game's replayability. Pulling off a perfect assassination is incredibly satisfying, making this a game I kept coming back to long after finishing the story.

Hitman: World of Assassination is one of the best stealth games I have played in a long time. The game gives players many different ways to complete missions, which makes it feel creative and fun instead of repetitive. Every map is large and detailed, and there are so many disguises, weapons, and hidden paths to discover. The freedom the game gives you makes each mission exciting because you can play however you want.

One of the best parts of the game is the level design. Places like Dubai, Miami, and Paris feel alive because there are crowds of people, secrets hidden everywhere, and multiple ways to reach your target. The game rewards patience and planning, but it can also be funny when things go wrong. Sometimes I would spend twenty minutes making the perfect plan, only for a random guard to ruin everything. Even when missions fail, it still feels entertaining.

The graphics and sound are also very impressive. The environments look realistic, and the voice acting helps bring the characters to life. Agent 47 is calm and serious, which matches the tone of the game perfectly. The music builds tension during important moments and makes sneaking around feel intense. I also like how the game mixes serious assassination missions with dark humor, making it stand out from other stealth games.

Overall, Hitman: World of Assassination is an amazing game for people who enjoy strategy, stealth, and replayability. There is always something new to try, whether it is a new disguise, challenge, or way to eliminate a target. Even after finishing the main story, the game still has plenty of content to keep players busy. I would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a smart and fun gaming experience.

Probably just my ♥♥♥♥♥♥ computer but the game wont load.
got it on sale for $8.71 NZD great price but if i try loading it, it just crashes.
I tried so many fixes but nothing. I could request a refund but I still have hope that i can play it someday on another device. 

I give this a Did not work/10

So excited for First Light. IOI know how to make an exotic location come to life with hundreds of NPCs going about their day, lots of little nooks and crannies to explore and use to your advantage, and generate a myriad of close shaves that feel so organic yet fully intentional. The Hitman reboot trilogy quietly becoming as celebrated as it is years on was fully deserved.
I'm not pro player, but the gameplay is so addictive. The visuals are amazing and the tutorial missions are incredibly helpful for figuring out the maps on your first try.
amazing game, some of the most fun i've ever had with a sandbox game. stealth isn't as in-depth as something like MGS, but the sheer amount of freedom the game gives you more than makes up for it.

Among the best games I have ever played! More than worth a buy, particularly if you compete in custom missions live against friends (e.g., by playing the same mission, at the same time, whilst in a Discord call) and always aim for perfection in the story -- silent assassin makes the game so much more enjoyable!

Hoping to write a comprehensive review later down the line!
The game is absolutely peak. Its so satisfying to just strangle someone you dont like. Highly recommend.

FALSE ADVERTISEMENT or clearly misleading. I suspect with intent. I bought this game because the series "World of Assassination" is advertised everywhere with VR SUPPORT. It has that tag on its store page and it even puts itself in the VR category in your library once you purchase it! After purchase and installation you wonder why it doesn't start in VR. Then you find on the store page somewhere the fineprint that 'Part One does not include VR mode' only when you happen to look at the 'comparison of editions'. Part One still has the VR tag and advertisement banners "VR Game of Year Award 2022" on the series' store page. It still sorts itself into the VR category in your library... as if. I uninstalled it and won't touch the series anymore. Clearly misleading. I'm willing to pay for games but sh*t like this pisses me off!
this game makes you feel like mater from cars 2 and you just ate a lot of wasabi thinking it was pistachio ice cream! Uh oh! Well good thing there is a big waterfall right up on stage!

9/10 would be a 10/10 if the stupid game had a easy way to get all the content. this game is so action packed and you can do stupid things still get the mission or do it with proper stealth its just such a versatile game. also credit where credit is due if i want to play this on my laptop i can they optimized it really well also god i love hitting men

Hitman W.o.A is a game where you... hit... men! Shocking isn't it. You also have tasks to hit women as well (inclusive).
Game plays smooth on a Steam Deck and it scratches that quiet intrusive itch everyone has. You know the one, to be a sneaky silent slick agent and do secret agent crime.
Overall:
GAMEPLAY 10/10 - Would hit many men again.
GRAPHICS 11/10 - ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. 
STORY 8/10 - Its peak but can use improvement
HIGHLY RECOMENDED
Just got it on sale and the servers are already down leaving me unable to play a single player game without always online access. What a joke.

tbh, this reminds me of Goldeneye from n64 (without the multiplayer aspect, obviously) and here's why:
the stages are episodic & repeatable (with the added benefit of save states). 
also each stage contains a Multitude of tasks that also unlock gear (so the replayability factor is there). 
and the story is mediocre at best (shallow, but enough to care a little bit about the characters).
but the gameplay is fun(!) and that is all that matters. i can disguise as a handyman, knock a homeowner out with a pipewrench, then snipe my target through their upstairs bedroom. or become a sushi chef and intentionally butcher a potentially poisonous fish for a target to eat.
the only downside (and its a big one) is that you must be online the whole time if you want any progress to be saved. so... beside that huge downside, i still think its worth it.
Constant crashes. From what I've read online, it seems to be a problem with the 9000 series amd cards.

Great games. But always online for unlockables in singleplayer is a shame, so thumbs down.

And yes, the serves go down every once in a while, locking you out. Even some settings (smuggled items) to start a game requires a online connection before even starting a game.

HITMAN World of Assassination is an amazing game. Though it's high price does drive some away, I highly recommend you pick it up while on sale. (I did not think about this and bought it at full price. Do not do what I did. It was not the smart choice for my wallet.) While its different versions can be confusing, even if you just get a version containing HITMAN 2016, the game's open-ended approaches to killing targets and sandbox nature allow great freedom and replayability that build off the best parts of the series. That, on top of the fact that the team at IO Interactive continues to update the game and make new Elusive Target missions on top of their work on 007 First Light, is very impressive and appreciated. Like, seriously, they put a HUGE amount of work into The Contagion, and knowing that they were working on First Light on top of that is even more impressive. So, yes, this game is worth the money, but I do recommend you buy it on sale, for your wallet's sake. However, my main problems come in the form of Freelancer.

HUGE RANT AHEAD
Freelancer is a rougelike gamemode where you take down a syndicate consisting of random NPCs from any map you own in the game. You start with no weapons or tools, and buy them with the money you get for completing missions, much like the earlier games in the franchise. It's fun, but my critiques are with the inability to restart a mission and the incentive to keep playing. You can't restart a mission once you start it in Freelancer. If you chose to exit to the menu, it counts as a loss. And when you lose, you lose any tools/weapons you took with you, and half of your money, as well as making all future locations in the current leg of your campaign harder to navigate. It's a rather steep cost for what was possibly one misstep or an unlucky spawn, so many people that play Freelancer just force quit the game when something goes wrong to avoid this. I feel like if it was like the Elusive Targets, where you could reset as many times as you want until any objective was completed, it would disincentivize people from doing so, allowing them to play around a bit and find the beginning of their route while still somewhat keeping the "one shot" feeling of Freelancer. My other complaint is the incentive to continue playing Freelancer besides just having fun. When you beat a mission, you get money, but most of the money will come from optional payout objectives, most of which need certain weapons or tools. You get these by buying them with the money you get from missions. So essentially, the cycle is get money, get weapons, use weapons to complete objectives, rinse and repeat. The problem is, buying weapons is the ONLY thing you can use money for. And once you have all the different types of weapons the payout objectives will need you to use, you don't really need any more. So they're just there to look cool, and the objective might be "get all the weapons." But once you do that, you can "prestige," turning in all your weapons, with the upside being that you now get more money from payout objectives. So now you've gotten rid of all your stuff... for the chance to get them back, but faster. I understand it would be hard to actually find a use for the money outside of buying weapons, and I personally can't think of a way, but maybe someone else can.

This isn't to say Freelancer isn't fun, it's nice if you just want to play some challenging Hitman, as since you're killing NPCs that weren't designed to be killed unnoticed and sneakily, it can be quite the challenge sometimes. But Freelancer's flaws are still there. A good addition to a great game, but I feel like it could be expanded on.

Hitman é um ótimo jogo, divertido pra caramba e um dos melhores jogos arcade que eu já joguei, ate porque a narrativa é bem fodase. Recomendo que vocês baixem o peacock Project pra poder acessar alguns conteúdos dos jogos passados e poderem também desfrutar do jogo mesmo depois que os servidores fechem. No geral, um ótimo jogo que vale cada centavo!! Só recomendo que depois de zerar o modo história vocês voltem nas fases pra fazer os desafios, farmar xp e desbloquear itens legais. Dei dislike porque a empresa é sebosa e tem preguiça de adicionar dublagem em português(que está presente nos outros dois jogos) e meh as dlcs são muito caras e se nao quiser dinheiro precisa de um literal tutorial pra comprar o jogo
This is very good game but is too expensive i buy one dlc and i need to buy a nother 100 dlc this game is soo expensive
Literally the best game ever idk what my life would be like without HITMAN

One of the best and most interactive game I have ever played. Hitman truly lives up to it's name and has fascinated me in lot ways that no one could even tell W storyline and game modes
Apparently they deduct points if you go after NPCs, absolutely crazy, that's my favorite part. But other than that it's a good game.

**This is my first time owning a Hitman game** At first I thought this game was worth the 8 dollars I spent on sale, it's not. 70% of everything in this game requires a $40 dollar upgrade pack. Scummy sales technique. Just can't recommend it because of that.

Hitman World of Assassination is one of my favorite games of all time. I've been playing video games since I was a kid, and the Hitman series has been with me for most of my life. This is easily the best entry in the franchise and probably one of the best games I have ever played. The level design, replayability, freedom of approach and attention to detail are simply outstanding.

However, I'm leaving a negative review because of IO Interactive's pricing and upgrade policy.

I bought Hitman 1 when it launched. Then I bought Hitman 2. Now, if I want to get Hitman 3 / World of Assassination, I'm expected to pay full price again, despite already owning and paying for the previous games. There is no meaningful discount or loyalty upgrade for long-time fans. Instead, I pay the exact same price as someone who has never purchased a Hitman game before.

That feels like a slap in the face to the people who supported the series from the beginning. Loyal customers should not be treated the same as first-time buyers when they've already invested heavily in the franchise over the years.

The game itself is fantastic, but IO Interactive's business practices in this regard feel like pure fan exploitation. It's a very disappointing and frankly disgusting way to treat your most dedicated players.

Amazing game. Terrible consumer treatment.
That's why this review is negative.

Hitman: World of Assassination is one of the best stealth games ever made. Every mission is a giant sandbox filled with countless ways to eliminate targets, from perfectly planned "accidents" to chaotic improvisation. The level design is phenomenal, encouraging experimentation and rewarding creativity. With over 20 unique locations, hundreds of challenges, and the endlessly replayable Freelancer mode, there's always something new to discover.

Agent 47 has never felt better to play. Whether you prefer silent assassinations, disguises, sniper shots, or complete mayhem, the game gives you the freedom to approach objectives however you want. The trilogy's content being unified into a single package makes it the definitive Hitman experience.

The only real downside is the always-online requirement for some features, but the incredible replayability, brilliant level design, and near-endless content easily outweigh that issue.

"The mission is always the same. How you complete it is entirely up to you." Agent 47 at his absolute best.

Kinda disappointing personally. Maybe the genre of stealth and assassination just isn't for me, but it gets repetitive the more levels you go through, like just wandering from point A to B. The level of detail for maps, characters, dialogue, graphics etc are great, but the gameplay itself can feel very slow and boring. It can take a lot of patience, and the success didn’t feel as rewarding compared to the time it took.

Rating: 4/10

Stick With Hitman 2. They give you Ads for ♥♥♥♥ sake every time I log in. I thought that was a no-no on Steam?... And getting HITMAN 3 or what is now called  "HITMAN: World of Assassination" for some reason; Is a confusing mess! Their sales titles and descriptions are misleading imo. DLC's out the ass. None of your past stuff will come over unless you buy the feature. It's a huge bloated mess that is aimed to milk me. I'm trapped. Don't follow suit.

It's a freaking confusing mess to buy the entire series & DLC.

But once you get past that, you have one of the all time greatest stealth experiences I've ever played. 

IOI were selected to make a 007 game based on how well the Hitman games, this trilogy in particular, capture the feelings of assassination & espionage.

Each level has a crazy amount of different ways to assassinate your targets, and as a result makes for one the most replayable games I've enjoyed.

11/10 gameplay experience.

3/10 buying experience for how confusing it can be to get all the main content.

Gameplay is so amazing most people don't even care enough to play or care for the campaign. This games story is by far the best of any I've ever played, through hitman 1 it seems a drag as you don't know where it's going but through 2 you realize what the goal is and during the 3rd game you don't know who is on your side if your going to win and you can't see how it will end and the ending is perfect.

This is probably the best assassin / spy game I've ever played. The maps are huge and absolutely beautiful to look at. The game gives you so much freedom and creativity to handle whatever situation you're in. Shoot, strangle, poison, stab, explode, distract, etc etc.

I almost exclusively play this game for the "Freelancer" mode. It's kind of like a roguelike(ish?) mode where you are given random NPCs to assassinate. It's stupid hard since the margin for error is pretty tight. One little mistake can cost you your entire run, literally. The randomness of the NPC contracts will force you to be creative with your assassinations. Once things kind of click and you get familiar with how the AI behavior works, it's so fun.

I have around maybe 60 - 70 hours across all consoles and I've yet to beat a full Freelancer run ( I suck, I know ), but it goes to show that I have fun with this game even if I don't win.

one of the greatest sandbox experiences out there. endless ways to tackle your objectives in many creative ways and that's without considering custom contracts or the amazing mode that is freelancer. for these reasons its also one of the most exciting and active speedrunning games out there. sure its expensive if you want all the content the game has to offer but ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ you can play this game forever.
one of the best stealth game , might be the best stealth game ever. I really love being a spy in this game [obviously].

Another one of the best 3rd person game made in the last 10yrs.
I've owned it on Ps4, 5, and xbox... Of course, the PC version is the best(in this case,) because: 
1. mods
2. steam controller(this could be number one as it really saved this game for me. the mouse works well 
for aiming, however)
      ---this thing will let you fix the atrocious aiming with controller in the default game. 
           Here's how:
-enable deadzone
-set to "custom deadzone"--remove all the deadzone factors(0-100)
-turn on "extra deadzone settings"
-enable "anti-deadzone"
-set "anti-deadzone to 14,000. (I know, but trust me.)
-set "sensitivity curve" to around 80-90...
-set sensitivity(in-game options) to MAX.
-play around and shoot things....it's finicky and highly sensitive on what you change, but it is 1.7million times better than NOT using this, or the console version...which is just, unfortunately: screwed.
3. shoulder switch that stays on either side of character
4. endlessly replayable
5. FOV is correct by default
6. runs REALLY well at high settings
7. you have to use brain BY DEFAULT. yea, it's unheard of now.
* buy everything for this game(all the locations, etc. dlc guns/suits at your discretion,) when it is ON SALE.... if it isn't rn, just wait, because it WILL BE...and soon.
* Go MOD IT NOW.
**My suggestions for mods(Nexus):
1. SMF----required for mods and also just a really good mod manager period. You can adjust everything for every mod...make sure to just drag all the mods into the SMF, adjust the settings AND THEN "deploy mods." otherwise, you will have to "deploy" again, and it takes a WHILE.
2. Spartan Mod
3. Lighting Ultimate
3. Hitman 2016 Movement
4. Hitman 3 Signature Suits Ties Upgraded
5. Realistic Snipers
6. Boomsticks -Shotgun Knockback
7. Impact Physics and Lock-on Adjustments
8. Silent Cameras
9. Immersive Sound
10. Handler earpiece dialogue
11. SPS-Caveira Edition
12. Magazine Count Configurator
13. No Hitmarkers
14. Accumulating Door Damage
15. No Poison Smoke
16. No Bullet Explosions
17. No Aiming Zoom
18. Minimalist HUD
19. No Camera Filters
20. Unofficial Community Patch
21. Accent Overhaul
**do not enable "Realistic AI," if you enable #1, as it is already in it.
These are all immersive mods that, in my opinion, make the game as it should have been. It is, even without these mods, a very good game.
Have fun, and hope this helps someone. :D

----------

   Short Version: It good.

----------

   Longer Version: 

   Hitman: World of Assassination is one of the easiest games I've ever recommended to people who enjoy stealth, sandbox gameplay, or simply getting their money's worth. Beyond the already lengthy main campaign—which includes the full stories of Hitman, Hitman 2, and Hitman 3—the game offers an incredible amount of replayability. Every location is a massive playground packed with opportunities, disguises, secrets, and mission stories. Completing a mission once often feels like you've only scratched the surface, and it's easy to spend dozens of hours revisiting maps to discover new assassination methods and challenges.

   The visuals have also aged remarkably well. The environments are detailed, vibrant, and packed with life, making each destination memorable. Whether you're infiltrating a crowded fashion show, a luxury resort, or a heavily guarded compound, the world consistently looks fantastic. The Freelancer mode is another standout feature, adding a roguelike twist that completely changes how you approach missions. It gives veteran players a fresh reason to revisit every map in the game and creates tense, rewarding gameplay where every decision matters.

   My biggest complaint is the handling of certain DLC content. Some content was only available during specific purchase windows, meaning that if you didn't buy it at the time, you can no longer obtain it. What makes this especially frustrating is that the game still displays this content in menus and even provides a "Gain Access" option, only to redirect you to the Steam store where you're informed that the content is no longer available. It's a disappointing practice in an otherwise outstanding package.

   Despite that issue, Hitman: World of Assassination remains one of the best stealth games available today. The sheer variety of approaches, enormous replay value, excellent Freelancer mode, and beautiful locations make it a game that can keep players entertained for hundreds of hours.

----------

Morbid but funny puzzle/stealth game, with an amazing story/universe/athmosphere. The gameplay used to consist of trying to do a challenege, getting caught ,going on a rampage and reloading the save. If you were not a completionist or a speedrunner most missions got kind of old after you played them 1-5 times. (escalations are fun aswell) After that the game was kind of done.

Then they added the roguelike/roguelite freelancer mode. Now the game doesent end when you are caught, and your practice mowing down entire maps comes in handy. The hours spent completing challenges give you knowledge about map traversal. Escalations help you complete challenges for money. Gadgets you never see in regular gameplay are suddenly great options because of resource scarcity. All sorts of systems that are actually fun and engaging like getting back confiscated items deleting security tapes, fake surrendering, keeping track of disguises and eyewitnesses actually matter. Freelancer is difficult enough (escaping targets) so you cant just shoot your way through everyting, and easy enough that you can get away with some mistakes.
It rewards creativity and taking risks, while showcasing every sytem thats hidden if you play savescum simulator. 
Its the perfect way to experience hitman. Very enjoyable.

It's a fun strategy game where that you can kill your targets however you want. Even when you finish the whole game there is a freelancer mode where it has random generated targets and you earn money to upgrade your safe house and buy more weapons to help you for your next target(s)
